   522‚Üí// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   523‚Üí// Hue picker ‚Äî horizontal gradient bar for selecting hue angle
   524‚Üí// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   525‚Üí
   526‚Üíconst HUE_PRESETS: { label: string; hue: number }[] = [
   527‚Üí  { label: "Red", hue: 0 },
   528‚Üí  { label: "Orange", hue: 30 },
   529‚Üí  { label: "Gold", hue: 45 },
   530‚Üí  { label: "Green", hue: 150 },
   531‚Üí  { label: "Teal", hue: 180 },
   532‚Üí  { label: "Blue", hue: 220 },
   533‚Üí  { label: "Indigo", hue: 245 },
   534‚Üí  { label: "Purple", hue: 280 },
   535‚Üí  { label: "Rose", hue: 340 },
   536‚Üí];
   537‚Üí
   538‚Üífunction HuePicker({
   539‚Üí  hue,
   540‚Üí  onChange,
   541‚Üí  onReset,
   542‚Üí  isDefault,
   543‚Üí}: {
   544‚Üí  hue: number;
   545‚Üí  onChange: (h: number) => void;
   546‚Üí  onReset: () => void;
   547‚Üí  isDefault: boolean;
   548‚Üí}) {
   549‚Üí  const barRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);
   550‚Üí
   551‚Üí  const hueFromPointer = useCallback((clientX: number) => {
   552‚Üí    const bar = barRef.current;
   553‚Üí    if (!bar) return;
   554‚Üí    const rect = bar.getBoundingClientRect();
   555‚Üí    const ratio = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, (clientX - rect.left) / rect.width));
   556‚Üí    onChange(Math.round(ratio * 360));
   557‚Üí  }, [onChange]);
   558‚Üí
   559‚Üí  const onPointerDown = useCallback((e: React.PointerEvent) => {
   560‚Üí    e.currentTarget.setPointerCapture(e.pointerId);
   561‚Üí    hueFromPointer(e.clientX);
   562‚Üí  }, [hueFromPointer]);
   563‚Üí
   564‚Üí  const onPointerMove = useCallback((e: React.PointerEvent) => {
   565‚Üí    if (e.buttons === 0) return;
   566‚Üí    hueFromPointer(e.clientX);
   567‚Üí  }, [hueFromPointer]);
   568‚Üí
   569‚Üí  return (
   570‚Üí    <div className="space-y-2">
   571‚Üí      {/* Presets + reset */}
   572‚Üí      <div className="flex items-center gap-1.5">
   573‚Üí        {HUE_PRESETS.map((p) => (
   574‚Üí          <button
   575‚Üí            key={p.hue}
   576‚Üí            onClick={() => onChange(p.hue)}
   577‚Üí            title={`${p.label} (${p.hue}¬∞)`}
   578‚Üí            className="h-5 flex-1 rounded-sm transition-shadow focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-white/50 focus-visible:ring-offset-1 focus-visible:ring-offset-[hsl(0_0%_10%)]"
   579‚Üí            style={{
   580‚Üí              backgroundColor: `hsl(${p.hue} 100% 50%)`,
   581‚Üí              boxShadow: hue === p.hue ? "0 0 0 1.5px hsl(0 0% 80%)" : "none",
   582‚Üí            }}
   583‚Üí          />
   584‚Üí        ))}
   585‚Üí
   586‚Üí        {/* Reset */}
   587‚Üí        <button
   588‚Üí          onClick={onReset}
   589‚Üí          title={`Reset to ${DEFAULT_HUE}¬∞`}
   590‚Üí          className="h-5 w-5 flex-none grid place-items-center rounded-sm transition-opacity focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-white/50 focus-visible:ring-offset-1 focus-visible:ring-offset-[hsl(0_0%_10%)]"
   591‚Üí          style={{ opacity: isDefault ? 0.25 : 0.7 }}
   592‚Üí        >
   593‚Üí          <svg width="12" height="12" viewBox="0 0 12 12" fill="none" className="text-[hsl(0_0%_60%)]">
   594‚Üí            <path
   595‚Üí              d="M2 2v3h3M10 10V7H7"
   596‚Üí              stroke="currentColor"
   597‚Üí              strokeWidth="1.2"
   598‚Üí              strokeLinecap="round"
   599‚Üí              strokeLinejoin="round"
   600‚Üí            />
   601‚Üí            <path
   602‚Üí              d="M9.5 4.5A4 4 0 0 0 3.17 2.83L2 4M2.5 7.5A4 4 0 0 0 8.83 9.17L10 8"
   603‚Üí              stroke="currentColor"
   604‚Üí              strokeWidth="1.2"
   605‚Üí              strokeLinecap="round"
   606‚Üí              strokeLinejoin="round"
   607‚Üí            />
   608‚Üí          </svg>
   609‚Üí        </button>
   610‚Üí      </div>
   611‚Üí
   612‚Üí      {/* Gradient bar */}
   613‚Üí      <div
   614‚Üí        ref={barRef}
   615‚Üí        role="slider"
   616‚Üí        tabIndex={0}
   617‚Üí        aria-valuemin={0}
   618‚Üí        aria-valuemax={360}
   619‚Üí        aria-valuenow={hue}
   620‚Üí        aria-label="Reference hue angle"
   621‚Üí        className="h-6 rounded-sm cursor-crosshair relative focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-white/50"
   622‚Üí        style={{
   623‚Üí          background: "linear-gradient(to right, hsl(0 100% 50%), hsl(60 100% 50%), hsl(120 100% 50%), hsl(180 100% 50%), hsl(240 100% 50%), hsl(300 100% 50%), hsl(360 100% 50%))",
   624‚Üí        }}
   625‚Üí        onPointerDown={onPointerDown}
   626‚Üí        onPointerMove={onPointerMove}
   627‚Üí        onKeyDown={(e) => {
   628‚Üí          const step = e.shiftKey ? 10 : 1;
   629‚Üí          let next: number | null = null;
   630‚Üí          if (e.key === "ArrowRight" || e.key === "ArrowUp") {
   631‚Üí            next = (hue + step) % 360;
   632‚Üí          } else if (e.key === "ArrowLeft" || e.key === "ArrowDown") {
   633‚Üí            next = (hue - step + 360) % 360;
   634‚Üí          }
   635‚Üí          if (next !== null) {
   636‚Üí            e.preventDefault();
   637‚Üí            onChange(next);
   638‚Üí          }
   639‚Üí        }}
   640‚Üí      >
   641‚Üí        <div
   642‚Üí          className="absolute top-0 h-full w-1 -translate-x-1/2 border border-white rounded-sm pointer-events-none"
   643‚Üí          style={{
   644‚Üí            left: `${(hue / 360) * 100}%`,
   645‚Üí            backgroundColor: `hsl(${hue} 100% 50%)`,
   646‚Üí          }}
   647‚Üí        />
   648‚Üí      </div>
   649‚Üí    </div>
   650‚Üí  );
   651‚Üí}

   657‚Üíexport function AboutButton() {

   684‚Üí            </Dialog.Overlay>

   686‚Üí              <motion.div
   687‚Üí                initial={{ opacity: 0, scale: 0.96, y: 8 }}
   688‚Üí                animate={{ opacity: 1, scale: 1, y: 0 }}
   689‚Üí                exit={{ opacity: 0, scale: 0.96, y: 8 }}
   690‚Üí                transition={{ duration: 0.2 }}
   691‚Üí                className="fixed left-1/2 top-1/2 -translate-x-1/2 -translate-y-1/2 z-50 w-full max-w-3xl p-6 rounded-2xl bg-[hsl(0_0%_6%)] border border-white/[0.08] shadow-2xl max-h-[85vh] overflow-y-auto"
   692‚Üí              >
   693‚Üí                <div className="grid grid-cols-[1fr_1.4fr] gap-x-6 gap-y-8">
   694‚Üí                  {/* Title + intro ‚Äî span both columns */}
   695‚Üí                  <div className="col-span-2">
   696‚Üí                    <Dialog.Title className="text-lg font-semibold text-white/90 mb-2">

   756‚Üí                        >

   793‚Üí                    <p className="text-[13px] leading-relaxed text-white/45">

   795‚Üí                    </p>
   796‚Üí                  </div>

   798‚Üí

   805‚Üí                    <p className="text-[13px] leading-relaxed text-white/45">

   822‚Üí                        {"Saturation: "}
   823‚Üí                        {Math.round(BOUNDARY_SCALE * getSaturation(LIGHTNESS_LABELS[0], REF_SMOD, "endpoint") * 100)}

   825‚Üí                        {Math.round(BOUNDARY_SCALE * getSaturation(LIGHTNESS_LABELS[4], REF_SMOD, "endpoint") * 100)}
   826‚Üí                        {"/"}
   827‚Üí                        {Math.round(BOUNDARY_SCALE * getSaturation(LIGHTNESS_LABELS[8], REF_SMOD, "endpoint") * 100)}%
   828‚Üí                      </span>
   829‚Üí                    </div>
   830‚Üí                    <p className="text-[13px] leading-relaxed text-white/45">
   831‚Üí                      Sampling saturation along the curve produces even
   832‚Üí                      perceived chroma, but is too saturated for neutrals.
   833‚Üí                    </p>
   834‚Üí                  </div>
   835‚Üí                  <Strip colors={strips.boundary} mark={markCurved} />
   836‚Üí
   837‚Üí                  {/* Final result strip */}
   838‚Üí                  <div>

   850‚Üí                    <p className="text-[13px] leading-relaxed text-white/45">

I have all the information needed. Here is the complete design plan.

---

## Color Token System Design for Chroma Curve

### Step 1: Computing Resolved Values

The base background is `hsl(0 0% 6%)` (lightness = 6%). White at opacity `a` blended over this background resolves to:

**Lightness = 6 + 94 * a**

For surface/border/stroke tokens (these physically blend with the background):

| Opacity | Computation | Resolved Lightness |
|---------|-------------|-------------------|
| 0.02 | 6 + 94*0.02 | **7.9%** (~8%) |
| 0.04 | 6 + 94*0.04 | **9.8%** (~10%) |
| 0.06 | 6 + 94*0.06 | **11.6%** (~12%) |
| 0.08 | 6 + 94*0.08 | **13.5%** (~14%) |
| 0.10 | 6 + 94*0.10 | **15.4%** (~15%) |
| 0.15 | 6 + 94*0.15 | **20.1%** (~20%) |
| 0.20 | 6 + 94*0.20 | **24.8%** (~25%) |
| 0.25 | 6 + 94*0.25 | **29.5%** (~30%) |
| 0.70 | 6 + 94*0.70 | **71.8%** (~72%) |

For text tokens, the color is white at the given opacity. Since these render as semi-transparent white over whatever is behind them, they effectively display as a gray. Against the 6% base, the resolved lightness follows the same formula, but since text sits on the base, we can express the token as the perceived gray:

| text-white/N | Effective gray lightness |
|--------------|------------------------|
| 90 | 90.6% (~91%) |
| 80 | 81.2% (~81%) |
| 70 | 71.8% (~72%) |
| 60 | 62.4% (~62%) |
| 55 | 57.7% (~58%) |
| 50 | 53% |
| 45 | 48.3% (~48%) |
| 40 | 43.6% (~44%) |
| 30 | 34.2% (~34%) |
| 25 | 29.5% (~30%) |

### Step 2: Consolidation Analysis

**Text colors** -- current 10 levels can be consolidated:

- **90** (headers, prominent values): Used for dialog titles, readout values, button text. Keep as primary.
- **80** (body text): Used for export preview code block. Merge with 90 -- difference is barely perceptible. Actually, 80 is used in only one place (ExportPreview code text), and it serves a distinct "readable but not prominent" role. Keep as secondary.
- **70** (section headers, hover states): Used for chart section titles, subsection headers, hover endpoints. Keep as a distinct tier.
- **60** (labels): Used for control labels ("Saturation", "Lightness"), subtitle text, formula highlights. Merge 55 into 60 -- the 2% lightness difference between 58% and 62% is imperceptible.
- **50** (links, interactive muted text): Used for link text, description body. Merge 45 into 50 -- 48% vs 53% is negligible.
- **40** (select elements, interactive dim): Used for dropdown selects, icon buttons. Keep.
- **30** (axis labels, footnotes): Used for chart axis text, footnote references, section summaries. Merge 25 into 30 -- 30% vs 34% is close enough, and both serve the same "barely there annotation" role.

That gives us **7 text tiers** -- but we can compress further. 80 is only used once and 70 is close. Let me check the semantic roles more carefully:

- `text-white/90`: Dialog titles, value readouts, button labels -- **primary text**
- `text-white/80`: Code block text -- can use 90 (code is already differentiated by font)
- `text-white/70`: Section headings in charts, subsection titles, hover state target -- **secondary text**
- `text-white/60 + 55`: Control labels, subtitle -- **tertiary / label text**
- `text-white/50 + 45`: Body paragraphs, descriptions, formula text -- **muted text**
- `text-white/40`: Dropdown triggers, icon buttons -- **subtle interactive**
- `text-white/30 + 25`: Axis labels, footnote numbers, annotations -- **faint text**

That is **6 text tokens**. This is lean and defensible.

**Background surfaces:**
- `bg-white/[0.02]` (8%): Card/panel background -- **surface-raised**
- `bg-white/[0.04]` (10%): Segmented control container, grid gap fill -- **surface-overlay**
- `bg-white/[0.06]` (12%): Hover background on icon buttons -- **surface-hover**
- `bg-white/10` (15%): Active button background -- **surface-active**
- `bg-white/15` (20%): Button hover state -- **surface-active-hover**
- `bg-white/20` (25%): Slider range fill -- **surface-slider**
- `bg-white/70` (72%): Toggle switch thumb -- keep as `bg-white/70` or define token

The toggle thumb at 70% is a one-off and arguably functional, not a surface. But let's include it since we want to eliminate all opacity patterns.

**Borders:**
- `border-white/[0.06]` (12%): Panel borders, section dividers, card borders -- **border-default**
- `border-white/[0.08]` (14%): Dialog borders, popover borders -- **border-elevated**
- `border-white/10` (15%): Hue swatch border -- close to elevated, can merge into border-elevated

Actually, `border-white/10` resolves to 15% and `border-white/[0.08]` resolves to 14%. These are 1% apart. Merge them into one token.

**SVG strokes:**
- `rgba(255,255,255,0.06)` (12%): Grid lines -- same as border-default
- `rgba(255,255,255,0.08)` (14%): Slider track inline style -- same as border-elevated
- `rgba(255,255,255,0.12)` (17%): Zero-line on chart -- **stroke-emphasis** (new, sits between border and visible)
- `rgba(255,255,255,0.20)` (25%): Hover ring on palette swatch -- same as surface-slider, reuse
- `rgba(255,255,255,0.25)` (30%): Toggle track "on" state -- **stroke-toggle**
- `rgba(255,255,255,0.40)` (44%): Unadjusted curve line -- **stroke-secondary**
- `rgba(255,255,255,0.70)` (72%): Primary curve line -- **stroke-primary**

### Step 3: Final Token Set

Targeting ~18 tokens:

**Text (6 tokens):**

| Token | HSL Value | Replaces |
|-------|-----------|----------|
| `--text-primary` | `hsl(0 0% 91%)` | `text-white/90`, `text-white/80` |
| `--text-secondary` | `hsl(0 0% 72%)` | `text-white/70` |
| `--text-tertiary` | `hsl(0 0% 60%)` | `text-white/60`, `text-white/55` |
| `--text-muted` | `hsl(0 0% 50%)` | `text-white/50`, `text-white/45` |
| `--text-subtle` | `hsl(0 0% 44%)` | `text-white/40` |
| `--text-faint` | `hsl(0 0% 32%)` | `text-white/30`, `text-white/25` |

**Surfaces (6 tokens):**

| Token | HSL Value | Replaces |
|-------|-----------|----------|
| `--surface-base` | `hsl(0 0% 6%)` | `bg-[hsl(0_0%_6%)]` |
| `--surface-raised` | `hsl(0 0% 8%)` | `bg-white/[0.02]` |
| `--surface-overlay` | `hsl(0 0% 10%)` | `bg-white/[0.04]`, `bg-[hsl(0_0%_10%)]` |
| `--surface-hover` | `hsl(0 0% 12%)` | `bg-white/[0.06]` |
| `--surface-active` | `hsl(0 0% 15%)` | `bg-white/10` |
| `--surface-active-hover` | `hsl(0 0% 20%)` | `bg-white/15`, `bg-white/20` |

Note: `bg-white/15` (20%) and `bg-white/20` (25%) are merged. Both serve "elevated interactive state" roles. The slider range at 20% is being slightly adjusted, but this is visually equivalent on a dark background.

**Borders (2 tokens):**

| Token | HSL Value | Replaces |
|-------|-----------|----------|
| `--border-default` | `hsl(0 0% 12%)` | `border-white/[0.06]` |
| `--border-elevated` | `hsl(0 0% 15%)` | `border-white/[0.08]`, `border-white/10`, `hover:ring-white/20` |

**Strokes (4 tokens):**

| Token | HSL Value | Replaces |
|-------|-----------|----------|
| `--stroke-grid` | `hsl(0 0% 12%)` | `rgba(255,255,255,0.06)` on grid lines |
| `--stroke-axis` | `hsl(0 0% 17%)` | `rgba(255,255,255,0.12)` zero-line |
| `--stroke-secondary` | `hsl(0 0% 44%)` | `rgba(255,255,255,0.4)` unadjusted curve |
| `--stroke-primary` | `hsl(0 0% 72%)` | `rgba(255,255,255,0.7)` main curve |

**Total: 18 tokens**

Items deliberately excluded (per constraints):
- `bg-black/60`, `bg-black/50` -- overlays on variable content, keep as-is
- `ring-white/50` -- focus rings, keep as-is  
- `shadow-[0_0_0_1px_rgba(0,0,0,0.3),0_2px_4px_rgba(0,0,0,0.4)]` -- shadows, keep as-is
- `bg-white/70` on toggle thumb -- this is a component accent, can use `--text-secondary` (same value 72%)
- `rgba(255,255,255,0.25)` toggle track on -- use `--surface-active-hover` inline or a dedicated `--toggle-track` if desired. At 30% vs 20%, these differ. Better to fold it into `--surface-active-hover` and bump to `hsl(0 0% 24%)` as a compromise, or keep as a one-off. Since the toggle is one component, I recommend leaving it inline with an explicit HSL value `hsl(0 0% 30%)` or adding a 19th token `--toggle-track: hsl(0 0% 30%)`. Let me keep it at 18 and handle it inline.
- `bg-[hsl(0_0%_8%/0.85)]` on ControlBar -- this uses transparency for backdrop-blur. It should reference `--surface-raised` but keep the `/0.85` alpha for the blur effect. This is fine in Tailwind: `bg-[hsl(0_0%_8%/0.85)]` or use `bg-surface-raised/85` if Tailwind 4 supports it.
- `rgba(255,255,255,0.08)` inline style on slider tracks -- replace with `--border-elevated` value (15%), close enough to 14%.

### Step 4: The `@theme` Block

```css
@import "tailwindcss";

@theme {
  --font-sans: "Geist Sans", ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif;
  --font-mono: "Geist Mono", ui-monospace, monospace;

  /* Text */
  --color-text-primary: hsl(0 0% 91%);
  --color-text-secondary: hsl(0 0% 72%);
  --color-text-tertiary: hsl(0 0% 60%);
  --color-text-muted: hsl(0 0% 50%);
  --color-text-subtle: hsl(0 0% 44%);
  --color-text-faint: hsl(0 0% 32%);

  /* Surfaces */
  --color-surface-base: hsl(0 0% 6%);
  --color-surface-raised: hsl(0 0% 8%);
  --color-surface-overlay: hsl(0 0% 10%);
  --color-surface-hover: hsl(0 0% 12%);
  --color-surface-active: hsl(0 0% 15%);
  --color-surface-active-hover: hsl(0 0% 20%);

  /* Borders */
  --color-border-default: hsl(0 0% 12%);
  --color-border-elevated: hsl(0 0% 15%);

  /* Strokes (for SVG, referenced as CSS custom properties) */
  --color-stroke-grid: hsl(0 0% 12%);
  --color-stroke-axis: hsl(0 0% 17%);
  --color-stroke-secondary: hsl(0 0% 44%);
  --color-stroke-primary: hsl(0 0% 72%);
}
```

In Tailwind CSS 4, `--color-*` tokens registered under `@theme` automatically generate utility classes. So `--color-text-primary` produces `text-text-primary`, `--color-surface-base` produces `bg-surface-base`, `--color-border-default` produces `border-border-default`, etc.

### Step 5: Full Mapping Table

**Text classes:**

| Old | New | Files |
|-----|-----|-------|
| `text-white/90` | `text-text-primary` | Header, ExportDialog, ExportPreview, About, LightnessRange, SliderBase, SaturationSlider |
| `text-white/80` | `text-text-primary` | ExportPreview (code block) |
| `text-white/70` | `text-text-secondary` | SaturationCurve, EffectiveSaturation, About (section heads, hover states) |
| `text-white/60` | `text-text-tertiary` | Header, About (formula highlights), SliderBase, LightnessRange, SaturationSlider |
| `text-white/55` | `text-text-tertiary` | PaletteGrid, Header, ToggleSwitch |
| `text-white/50` | `text-text-muted` | About (descriptions, links), FormatSelector, ExportDialog |
| `text-white/45` | `text-text-muted` | About (body paragraphs, formula) |
| `text-white/40` | `text-text-subtle` | About (icon buttons, formula param), PaletteGrid (selects), ExportDialog |
| `text-white/30` | `text-text-faint` | SaturationCurve (axis, details), EffectiveSaturation (axis), About (footnotes), FormatSelector |
| `text-white/25` | `text-text-faint` | About (superscript footnote refs) |

**SVG fill classes:**

| Old | New |
|-----|-----|
| `fill-white/30` | `fill-text-faint` |

**Background classes:**

| Old | New |
|-----|-----|
| `bg-[hsl(0_0%_6%)]` | `bg-surface-base` |
| `bg-white/[0.02]` | `bg-surface-raised` |
| `bg-white/[0.04]` | `bg-surface-overlay` |
| `bg-[hsl(0_0%_10%)]` | `bg-surface-overlay` |
| `bg-white/[0.06]` (hover) | `bg-surface-hover` |
| `bg-white/10` | `bg-surface-active` |
| `bg-white/15` | `bg-surface-active-hover` |
| `bg-white/20` | `bg-surface-active-hover` |

**Border classes:**

| Old | New |
|-----|-----|
| `border-white/[0.06]` | `border-border-default` |
| `border-white/[0.08]` | `border-border-elevated` |
| `border-white/10` | `border-border-elevated` |

**SVG stroke attributes (inline):**

| Old | New |
|-----|-----|
| `stroke="rgba(255,255,255,0.06)"` | `stroke="var(--color-stroke-grid)"` |
| `stroke="rgba(255,255,255,0.12)"` | `stroke="var(--color-stroke-axis)"` |
| `stroke="rgba(255,255,255,0.20)"` | `stroke="var(--color-stroke-axis)"` (close at 17% vs 25% -- or use `var(--color-border-elevated)`) |
| `stroke="rgba(255,255,255,0.4)"` | `stroke="var(--color-stroke-secondary)"` |
| `stroke="rgba(255,255,255,0.7)"` | `stroke="var(--color-stroke-primary)"` |

**Inline styles:**

| Old | New |
|-----|-----|
| `backgroundColor: "rgba(255 255 255 / 0.08)"` (slider tracks) | `backgroundColor: "var(--color-border-elevated)"` or use class `bg-surface-active` |
| `"rgba(255,255,255,0.25)"` (toggle on) | `"var(--color-surface-active-hover)"` or `hsl(0 0% 24%)` inline |
| `"rgba(255,255,255,0.06)"` (toggle off) | `"var(--color-surface-hover)"` |
| `bg-[hsl(0_0%_8%/0.85)]` (ControlBar) | `bg-surface-raised/85` (Tailwind 4 alpha modifier on custom color) |
| `focus-visible:ring-offset-[hsl(0_0%_6%)]` | `focus-visible:ring-offset-surface-base` |
| `focus-visible:ring-offset-[hsl(0_0%_10%)]` | `focus-visible:ring-offset-surface-overlay` |

**Items left as-is:**

| Pattern | Reason |
|---------|--------|
| `bg-black/60`, `bg-black/50` | Overlays on variable content |
| `ring-white/50` | Focus ring accessibility |
| `shadow-[..rgba(0,0,0,..)]` | Shadows need true opacity |
| `bg-white/70` on toggle thumb | Use `text-secondary` value (same 72%) via `bg-text-secondary` |
| `boxShadow: "0 0 0 1.5px hsl(0 0% 80%)"` | One-off selection indicator, already explicit HSL |

### Step 6: Implementation Sequence

1. **Add tokens to `app/src/index.css`** -- add the `@theme` block with all `--color-*` tokens. Remove the existing `--color-accent` (unused in components).

2. **Convert text classes** -- find-and-replace across all components. Start with the most-used patterns: `text-white/90` to `text-text-primary`, `text-white/70` to `text-text-secondary`, etc. Handle the `fill-white/30` SVG fills simultaneously.

3. **Convert background classes** -- replace `bg-white/[0.02]` with `bg-surface-raised`, etc. The ControlBar backdrop-blur case needs `bg-surface-raised/85` syntax (verify Tailwind 4 supports alpha modifiers on custom theme colors -- it does, via `bg-surface-raised/85`).

4. **Convert border classes** -- replace `border-white/[0.06]` with `border-border-default`, etc.

5. **Convert SVG stroke inline attributes** -- replace `stroke="rgba(...)"` with `stroke="var(--color-stroke-grid)"` etc.

6. **Convert inline styles** -- replace `backgroundColor: "rgba(255 255 255 / 0.08)"` in SliderBase, SaturationSlider with either the CSS variable or a Tailwind class.

7. **Convert the toggle switch** -- replace the inline rgba values with CSS variable references.

8. **Visual regression check** -- compare before/after screenshots. The differences should be imperceptible except where tokens were deliberately consolidated (e.g. text-white/55 becoming 60%, a 2% shift).

### Edge Cases and Risks

- **ControlBar transparency**: `bg-[hsl(0_0%_8%/0.85)]` uses alpha for backdrop-blur. The token `--surface-raised` is `hsl(0 0% 8%)` without alpha. In Tailwind 4, `bg-surface-raised/85` should apply 85% opacity to the token color. Verify this works.

- **`hover:text-white/70`** patterns: These are hover states that pair with a default state. For example, About.tsx line 867 has `text-white/50 hover:text-white/70` on links. This becomes `text-text-muted hover:text-text-secondary`. The semantic names still read well.

- **`bg-white/[0.04]` as grid gap fill**: PaletteGrid uses this as the visible gap color between swatches when borders are on. It becomes `bg-surface-overlay`. This is correct since the 1px gap reveals the grid's background color, simulating borders.

- **`stroke="rgba(255,255,255,0.20)"` on EffectiveSaturation**: This is used for a reference line. At 25% lightness, it sits between `--stroke-axis` (17%) and `--stroke-secondary` (44%). Using `--color-border-elevated` (15%) would make it dimmer. Consider adding `--stroke-reference: hsl(0 0% 25%)` if visual fidelity matters, or use `--stroke-axis`. Since this is one usage in one chart, using `--stroke-axis` and accepting the slight dimming is fine for a lean token set.

### Critical Files for Implementation
- /Users/grant/code/chroma-curve/app/src/index.css
- /Users/grant/code/chroma-curve/app/src/components/About.tsx
- /Users/grant/code/chroma-curve/app/src/components/controls/SliderBase.tsx
- /Users/grant/code/chroma-curve/app/src/components/curves/SaturationCurve.tsx

_**Agent - sidechain (2026-03-26 21:42:04Z)**_

I'm working on Chroma Curve, a dark-themed color palette generator built with React 19 + Tailwind CSS 4 + Vite.

## Task
Design a CSS custom property system to replace all opacity-based colors (white/XX, rgba(255,255,255,...), bg-white/[0.XX]) with explicit HSL gray values. This is a foundation-first design polish pass.

## Current State
The app uses `hsl(0 0% 6%)` as the base background. All text, borders, backgrounds, and SVG strokes use white-with-opacity patterns. Here's the full inventory of unique opacity levels in use:

**Text colors (text-white/XX):**
- 90, 80, 70, 60, 55, 50, 45, 40, 30, 25

**Background colors (bg-white/XX):**
- 70, 20, 15, 10, [0.06], [0.04], [0.02]

**Border colors (border-white/XX):**
- 10, [0.08], [0.06]

**SVG strokes (rgba):**
- 0.7, 0.4, 0.25, 0.2, 0.12, 0.08, 0.06

**Overlays (bg-black/XX):**
- 60, 50

**Shadows:**
- rgba(0,0,0,0.3), rgba(0,0,0,0.4)

## Constraints
- Tailwind CSS 4 (uses `@theme` directive for custom properties, not `theme.extend`)
- The app background is `hsl(0 0% 6%)`
- Design principle: "precise, minimal, sharp" ‚Äî the token system should be lean, not exhaustive
- Overlays (bg-black/60) and shadows can stay as opacity since they overlay variable content
- Focus rings (ring-white/50) can stay as-is since they're browser-level accessibility features
- The generated palette colors are the ONLY chromatic elements ‚Äî all UI is achromatic grays

## Design Requirements
1. Map the ~10 text opacity levels to a smaller set of semantic gray tokens (consolidate where possible ‚Äî do we really need both 55 and 60?)
2. Map background opacities to explicit gray values (compute what white/[0.02] actually resolves to on a 6% lightness background)
3. Map border opacities similarly
4. Map SVG stroke values
5. Name tokens semantically (e.g. `--text-primary`, `--text-muted`, `--border-subtle`) rather than by value
6. Show the Tailwind 4 `@theme` configuration
7. Keep the total token count tight ‚Äî aim for ~15-20 tokens max across all categories

Please provide:
- The recommended token set with names, HSL values, and which current opacity values they replace
- The `@theme` block for index.css
- A mapping table: old value ‚Üí new token

Now I have a complete picture. Let me compute the actual resolved HSL values for each opacity level against the `hsl(0 0% 6%)` background.

**Math for white-on-6% background:**
- White at opacity X on a 6% lightness background = `6% + (100% - 6%) * X = 6 + 94*X`
- For text: the color itself is what matters (not blended with background)
- For backgrounds/borders/strokes: these blend with the 6% base

Let me now organize all the actual usage and design the token system.

---

## Color Token System Design for Chroma Curve

### Step 1: Computing Resolved Values

The base background is `hsl(0 0% 6%)` (lightness = 6%). White at opacity `a` blended over this background resolves to:

**Lightness = 6 + 94 * a**

For surface/border/stroke tokens (these physically blend with the background):

| Opacity | Computation | Resolved Lightness |
|---------|-------------|-------------------|
| 0.02 | 6 + 94*0.02 | **7.9%** (~8%) |
| 0.04 | 6 + 94*0.04 | **9.8%** (~10%) |
| 0.06 | 6 + 94*0.06 | **11.6%** (~12%) |
| 0.08 | 6 + 94*0.08 | **13.5%** (~14%) |
| 0.10 | 6 + 94*0.10 | **15.4%** (~15%) |
| 0.15 | 6 + 94*0.15 | **20.1%** (~20%) |
| 0.20 | 6 + 94*0.20 | **24.8%** (~25%) |
| 0.25 | 6 + 94*0.25 | **29.5%** (~30%) |
| 0.70 | 6 + 94*0.70 | **71.8%** (~72%) |

For text tokens, the color is white at the given opacity. Since these render as semi-transparent white over whatever is behind them, they effectively display as a gray. Against the 6% base, the resolved lightness follows the same formula, but since text sits on the base, we can express the token as the perceived gray:

| text-white/N | Effective gray lightness |
|--------------|------------------------|
| 90 | 90.6% (~91%) |
| 80 | 81.2% (~81%) |
| 70 | 71.8% (~72%) |
| 60 | 62.4% (~62%) |
| 55 | 57.7% (~58%) |
| 50 | 53% |
| 45 | 48.3% (~48%) |
| 40 | 43.6% (~44%) |
| 30 | 34.2% (~34%) |
| 25 | 29.5% (~30%) |

### Step 2: Consolidation Analysis

**Text colors** -- current 10 levels can be consolidated:

- **90** (headers, prominent values): Used for dialog titles, readout values, button text. Keep as primary.
- **80** (body text): Used for export preview code block. Merge with 90 -- difference is barely perceptible. Actually, 80 is used in only one place (ExportPreview code text), and it serves a distinct "readable but not prominent" role. Keep as secondary.
- **70** (section headers, hover states): Used for chart section titles, subsection headers, hover endpoints. Keep as a distinct tier.
- **60** (labels): Used for control labels ("Saturation", "Lightness"), subtitle text, formula highlights. Merge 55 into 60 -- the 2% lightness difference between 58% and 62% is imperceptible.
- **50** (links, interactive muted text): Used for link text, description body. Merge 45 into 50 -- 48% vs 53% is negligible.
- **40** (select elements, interactive dim): Used for dropdown selects, icon buttons. Keep.
- **30** (axis labels, footnotes): Used for chart axis text, footnote references, section summaries. Merge 25 into 30 -- 30% vs 34% is close enough, and both serve the same "barely there annotation" role.

That gives us **7 text tiers** -- but we can compress further. 80 is only used once and 70 is close. Let me check the semantic roles more carefully:

- `text-white/90`: Dialog titles, value readouts, button labels -- **primary text**
- `text-white/80`: Code block text -- can use 90 (code is already differentiated by font)
- `text-white/70`: Section headings in charts, subsection titles, hover state target -- **secondary text**
- `text-white/60 + 55`: Control labels, subtitle -- **tertiary / label text**
- `text-white/50 + 45`: Body paragraphs, descriptions, formula text -- **muted text**
- `text-white/40`: Dropdown triggers, icon buttons -- **subtle interactive**
- `text-white/30 + 25`: Axis labels, footnote numbers, annotations -- **faint text**

That is **6 text tokens**. This is lean and defensible.

**Background surfaces:**
- `bg-white/[0.02]` (8%): Card/panel background -- **surface-raised**
- `bg-white/[0.04]` (10%): Segmented control container, grid gap fill -- **surface-overlay**
- `bg-white/[0.06]` (12%): Hover background on icon buttons -- **surface-hover**
- `bg-white/10` (15%): Active button background -- **surface-active**
- `bg-white/15` (20%): Button hover state -- **surface-active-hover**
- `bg-white/20` (25%): Slider range fill -- **surface-slider**
- `bg-white/70` (72%): Toggle switch thumb -- keep as `bg-white/70` or define token

The toggle thumb at 70% is a one-off and arguably functional, not a surface. But let's include it since we want to eliminate all opacity patterns.

**Borders:**
- `border-white/[0.06]` (12%): Panel borders, section dividers, card borders -- **border-default**
- `border-white/[0.08]` (14%): Dialog borders, popover borders -- **border-elevated**
- `border-white/10` (15%): Hue swatch border -- close to elevated, can merge into border-elevated

Actually, `border-white/10` resolves to 15% and `border-white/[0.08]` resolves to 14%. These are 1% apart. Merge them into one token.

**SVG strokes:**
- `rgba(255,255,255,0.06)` (12%): Grid lines -- same as border-default
- `rgba(255,255,255,0.08)` (14%): Slider track inline style -- same as border-elevated
- `rgba(255,255,255,0.12)` (17%): Zero-line on chart -- **stroke-emphasis** (new, sits between border and visible)
- `rgba(255,255,255,0.20)` (25%): Hover ring on palette swatch -- same as surface-slider, reuse
- `rgba(255,255,255,0.25)` (30%): Toggle track "on" state -- **stroke-toggle**
- `rgba(255,255,255,0.40)` (44%): Unadjusted curve line -- **stroke-secondary**
- `rgba(255,255,255,0.70)` (72%): Primary curve line -- **stroke-primary**

### Step 3: Final Token Set

Targeting ~18 tokens:

**Text (6 tokens):**

| Token | HSL Value | Replaces |
|-------|-----------|----------|
| `--text-primary` | `hsl(0 0% 91%)` | `text-white/90`, `text-white/80` |
| `--text-secondary` | `hsl(0 0% 72%)` | `text-white/70` |
| `--text-tertiary` | `hsl(0 0% 60%)` | `text-white/60`, `text-white/55` |
| `--text-muted` | `hsl(0 0% 50%)` | `text-white/50`, `text-white/45` |
| `--text-subtle` | `hsl(0 0% 44%)` | `text-white/40` |
| `--text-faint` | `hsl(0 0% 32%)` | `text-white/30`, `text-white/25` |

**Surfaces (6 tokens):**

| Token | HSL Value | Replaces |
|-------|-----------|----------|
| `--surface-base` | `hsl(0 0% 6%)` | `bg-[hsl(0_0%_6%)]` |
| `--surface-raised` | `hsl(0 0% 8%)` | `bg-white/[0.02]` |
| `--surface-overlay` | `hsl(0 0% 10%)` | `bg-white/[0.04]`, `bg-[hsl(0_0%_10%)]` |
| `--surface-hover` | `hsl(0 0% 12%)` | `bg-white/[0.06]` |
| `--surface-active` | `hsl(0 0% 15%)` | `bg-white/10` |
| `--surface-active-hover` | `hsl(0 0% 20%)` | `bg-white/15`, `bg-white/20` |

Note: `bg-white/15` (20%) and `bg-white/20` (25%) are merged. Both serve "elevated interactive state" roles. The slider range at 20% is being slightly adjusted, but this is visually equivalent on a dark background.

**Borders (2 tokens):**

| Token | HSL Value | Replaces |
|-------|-----------|----------|
| `--border-default` | `hsl(0 0% 12%)` | `border-white/[0.06]` |
| `--border-elevated` | `hsl(0 0% 15%)` | `border-white/[0.08]`, `border-white/10`, `hover:ring-white/20` |

**Strokes (4 tokens):**

| Token | HSL Value | Replaces |
|-------|-----------|----------|
| `--stroke-grid` | `hsl(0 0% 12%)` | `rgba(255,255,255,0.06)` on grid lines |
| `--stroke-axis` | `hsl(0 0% 17%)` | `rgba(255,255,255,0.12)` zero-line |
| `--stroke-secondary` | `hsl(0 0% 44%)` | `rgba(255,255,255,0.4)` unadjusted curve |
| `--stroke-primary` | `hsl(0 0% 72%)` | `rgba(255,255,255,0.7)` main curve |

**Total: 18 tokens**

Items deliberately excluded (per constraints):
- `bg-black/60`, `bg-black/50` -- overlays on variable content, keep as-is
- `ring-white/50` -- focus rings, keep as-is  
- `shadow-[0_0_0_1px_rgba(0,0,0,0.3),0_2px_4px_rgba(0,0,0,0.4)]` -- shadows, keep as-is
- `bg-white/70` on toggle thumb -- this is a component accent, can use `--text-secondary` (same value 72%)
- `rgba(255,255,255,0.25)` toggle track on -- use `--surface-active-hover` inline or a dedicated `--toggle-track` if desired. At 30% vs 20%, these differ. Better to fold it into `--surface-active-hover` and bump to `hsl(0 0% 24%)` as a compromise, or keep as a one-off. Since the toggle is one component, I recommend leaving it inline with an explicit HSL value `hsl(0 0% 30%)` or adding a 19th token `--toggle-track: hsl(0 0% 30%)`. Let me keep it at 18 and handle it inline.
- `bg-[hsl(0_0%_8%/0.85)]` on ControlBar -- this uses transparency for backdrop-blur. It should reference `--surface-raised` but keep the `/0.85` alpha for the blur effect. This is fine in Tailwind: `bg-[hsl(0_0%_8%/0.85)]` or use `bg-surface-raised/85` if Tailwind 4 supports it.
- `rgba(255,255,255,0.08)` inline style on slider tracks -- replace with `--border-elevated` value (15%), close enough to 14%.

### Step 4: The `@theme` Block

```css
@import "tailwindcss";

@theme {
  --font-sans: "Geist Sans", ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif;
  --font-mono: "Geist Mono", ui-monospace, monospace;

  /* Text */
  --color-text-primary: hsl(0 0% 91%);
  --color-text-secondary: hsl(0 0% 72%);
  --color-text-tertiary: hsl(0 0% 60%);
  --color-text-muted: hsl(0 0% 50%);
  --color-text-subtle: hsl(0 0% 44%);
  --color-text-faint: hsl(0 0% 32%);

  /* Surfaces */
  --color-surface-base: hsl(0 0% 6%);
  --color-surface-raised: hsl(0 0% 8%);
  --color-surface-overlay: hsl(0 0% 10%);
  --color-surface-hover: hsl(0 0% 12%);
  --color-surface-active: hsl(0 0% 15%);
  --color-surface-active-hover: hsl(0 0% 20%);

  /* Borders */
  --color-border-default: hsl(0 0% 12%);
  --color-border-elevated: hsl(0 0% 15%);

  /* Strokes (for SVG, referenced as CSS custom properties) */
  --color-stroke-grid: hsl(0 0% 12%);
  --color-stroke-axis: hsl(0 0% 17%);
  --color-stroke-secondary: hsl(0 0% 44%);
  --color-stroke-primary: hsl(0 0% 72%);
}
```

In Tailwind CSS 4, `--color-*` tokens registered under `@theme` automatically generate utility classes. So `--color-text-primary` produces `text-text-primary`, `--color-surface-base` produces `bg-surface-base`, `--color-border-default` produces `border-border-default`, etc.

### Step 5: Full Mapping Table

**Text classes:**

| Old | New | Files |
|-----|-----|-------|
| `text-white/90` | `text-text-primary` | Header, ExportDialog, ExportPreview, About, LightnessRange, SliderBase, SaturationSlider |
| `text-white/80` | `text-text-primary` | ExportPreview (code block) |
| `text-white/70` | `text-text-secondary` | SaturationCurve, EffectiveSaturation, About (section heads, hover states) |
| `text-white/60` | `text-text-tertiary` | Header, About (formula highlights), SliderBase, LightnessRange, SaturationSlider |
| `text-white/55` | `text-text-tertiary` | PaletteGrid, Header, ToggleSwitch |
| `text-white/50` | `text-text-muted` | About (descriptions, links), FormatSelector, ExportDialog |
| `text-white/45` | `text-text-muted` | About (body paragraphs, formula) |
| `text-white/40` | `text-text-subtle` | About (icon buttons, formula param), PaletteGrid (selects), ExportDialog |
| `text-white/30` | `text-text-faint` | SaturationCurve (axis, details), EffectiveSaturation (axis), About (footnotes), FormatSelector |
| `text-white/25` | `text-text-faint` | About (superscript footnote refs) |

**SVG fill classes:**

| Old | New |
|-----|-----|
| `fill-white/30` | `fill-text-faint` |

**Background classes:**

| Old | New |
|-----|-----|
| `bg-[hsl(0_0%_6%)]` | `bg-surface-base` |
| `bg-white/[0.02]` | `bg-surface-raised` |
| `bg-white/[0.04]` | `bg-surface-overlay` |
| `bg-[hsl(0_0%_10%)]` | `bg-surface-overlay` |
| `bg-white/[0.06]` (hover) | `bg-surface-hover` |
| `bg-white/10` | `bg-surface-active` |
| `bg-white/15` | `bg-surface-active-hover` |
| `bg-white/20` | `bg-surface-active-hover` |

**Border classes:**

| Old | New |
|-----|-----|
| `border-white/[0.06]` | `border-border-default` |
| `border-white/[0.08]` | `border-border-elevated` |
| `border-white/10` | `border-border-elevated` |

**SVG stroke attributes (inline):**

| Old | New |
|-----|-----|
| `stroke="rgba(255,255,255,0.06)"` | `stroke="var(--color-stroke-grid)"` |
| `stroke="rgba(255,255,255,0.12)"` | `stroke="var(--color-stroke-axis)"` |
| `stroke="rgba(255,255,255,0.20)"` | `stroke="var(--color-stroke-axis)"` (close at 17% vs 25% -- or use `var(--color-border-elevated)`) |
| `stroke="rgba(255,255,255,0.4)"` | `stroke="var(--color-stroke-secondary)"` |
| `stroke="rgba(255,255,255,0.7)"` | `stroke="var(--color-stroke-primary)"` |

**Inline styles:**

| Old | New |
|-----|-----|
| `backgroundColor: "rgba(255 255 255 / 0.08)"` (slider tracks) | `backgroundColor: "var(--color-border-elevated)"` or use class `bg-surface-active` |
| `"rgba(255,255,255,0.25)"` (toggle on) | `"var(--color-surface-active-hover)"` or `hsl(0 0% 24%)` inline |
| `"rgba(255,255,255,0.06)"` (toggle off) | `"var(--color-surface-hover)"` |
| `bg-[hsl(0_0%_8%/0.85)]` (ControlBar) | `bg-surface-raised/85` (Tailwind 4 alpha modifier on custom color) |
| `focus-visible:ring-offset-[hsl(0_0%_6%)]` | `focus-visible:ring-offset-surface-base` |
| `focus-visible:ring-offset-[hsl(0_0%_10%)]` | `focus-visible:ring-offset-surface-overlay` |

**Items left as-is:**

| Pattern | Reason |
|---------|--------|
| `bg-black/60`, `bg-black/50` | Overlays on variable content |
| `ring-white/50` | Focus ring accessibility |
| `shadow-[..rgba(0,0,0,..)]` | Shadows need true opacity |
| `bg-white/70` on toggle thumb | Use `text-secondary` value (same 72%) via `bg-text-secondary` |
| `boxShadow: "0 0 0 1.5px hsl(0 0% 80%)"` | One-off selection indicator, already explicit HSL |

### Step 6: Implementation Sequence

1. **Add tokens to `app/src/index.css`** -- add the `@theme` block with all `--color-*` tokens. Remove the existing `--color-accent` (unused in components).

2. **Convert text classes** -- find-and-replace across all components. Start with the most-used patterns: `text-white/90` to `text-text-primary`, `text-white/70` to `text-text-secondary`, etc. Handle the `fill-white/30` SVG fills simultaneously.

3. **Convert background classes** -- replace `bg-white/[0.02]` with `bg-surface-raised`, etc. The ControlBar backdrop-blur case needs `bg-surface-raised/85` syntax (verify Tailwind 4 supports alpha modifiers on custom theme colors -- it does, via `bg-surface-raised/85`).

4. **Convert border classes** -- replace `border-white/[0.06]` with `border-border-default`, etc.

5. **Convert SVG stroke inline attributes** -- replace `stroke="rgba(...)"` with `stroke="var(--color-stroke-grid)"` etc.

6. **Convert inline styles** -- replace `backgroundColor: "rgba(255 255 255 / 0.08)"` in SliderBase, SaturationSlider with either the CSS variable or a Tailwind class.

7. **Convert the toggle switch** -- replace the inline rgba values with CSS variable references.

8. **Visual regression check** -- compare before/after screenshots. The differences should be imperceptible except where tokens were deliberately consolidated (e.g. text-white/55 becoming 60%, a 2% shift).

### Edge Cases and Risks

- **ControlBar transparency**: `bg-[hsl(0_0%_8%/0.85)]` uses alpha for backdrop-blur. The token `--surface-raised` is `hsl(0 0% 8%)` without alpha. In Tailwind 4, `bg-surface-raised/85` should apply 85% opacity to the token color. Verify this works.

- **`hover:text-white/70`** patterns: These are hover states that pair with a default state. For example, About.tsx line 867 has `text-white/50 hover:text-white/70` on links. This becomes `text-text-muted hover:text-text-secondary`. The semantic names still read well.

- **`bg-white/[0.04]` as grid gap fill**: PaletteGrid uses this as the visible gap color between swatches when borders are on. It becomes `bg-surface-overlay`. This is correct since the 1px gap reveals the grid's background color, simulating borders.

- **`stroke="rgba(255,255,255,0.20)"` on EffectiveSaturation**: This is used for a reference line. At 25% lightness, it sits between `--stroke-axis` (17%) and `--stroke-secondary` (44%). Using `--color-border-elevated` (15%) would make it dimmer. Consider adding `--stroke-reference: hsl(0 0% 25%)` if visual fidelity matters, or use `--stroke-axis`. Since this is one usage in one chart, using `--stroke-axis` and accepting the slight dimming is fine for a lean token set.

- /Users/grant/code/chroma-curve/app/src/components/About.tsx
- /Users/grant/code/chroma-curve/app/src/components/controls/SliderBase.tsx
- /Users/grant/code/chroma-curve/app/src/components/curves/SaturationCurve.tsx
- /Users/grant/code/chroma-curve/app/src/components/export/ExportDialog.tsx

# Design Polish: Foundation ‚Äî Color Token System

## Context

All UI colors in Chroma Curve use white-with-opacity patterns (`text-white/60`, `bg-white/[0.02]`, `rgba(255,255,255,0.06)`). This was inherited from early development, not intentional. Opacity-based text colors blend unpredictably with backgrounds and make the design system implicit rather than explicit. This pass defines a CSS custom property system with explicit HSL gray values, then migrates all components.

## Token Set (18 tokens)

### Text (6)

| Token | Value | Replaces |
|-------|-------|----------|
| `--color-text-primary` | `hsl(0 0% 91%)` | `text-white/90`, `text-white/80` |
| `--color-text-secondary` | `hsl(0 0% 72%)` | `text-white/70` |
| `--color-text-tertiary` | `hsl(0 0% 60%)` | `text-white/60`, `text-white/55` |
| `--color-text-muted` | `hsl(0 0% 50%)` | `text-white/50`, `text-white/45` |
| `--color-text-subtle` | `hsl(0 0% 44%)` | `text-white/40` |
| `--color-text-faint` | `hsl(0 0% 32%)` | `text-white/30`, `text-white/25` |

### Surfaces (6)

| Token | Value | Replaces |
|-------|-------|----------|
| `--color-surface-base` | `hsl(0 0% 6%)` | `bg-[hsl(0_0%_6%)]` |
| `--color-surface-raised` | `hsl(0 0% 8%)` | `bg-white/[0.02]` |
| `--color-surface-overlay` | `hsl(0 0% 10%)` | `bg-white/[0.04]`, `bg-[hsl(0_0%_10%)]` |
| `--color-surface-hover` | `hsl(0 0% 12%)` | `bg-white/[0.06]` |
| `--color-surface-active` | `hsl(0 0% 15%)` | `bg-white/10` |
| `--color-surface-active-hover` | `hsl(0 0% 20%)` | `bg-white/15`, `bg-white/20` |

### Borders (2)

| Token | Value | Replaces |
|-------|-------|----------|
| `--color-border-default` | `hsl(0 0% 12%)` | `border-white/[0.06]` |
| `--color-border-elevated` | `hsl(0 0% 15%)` | `border-white/[0.08]`, `border-white/10` |

### Strokes (4)

| Token | Value | Replaces |
|-------|-------|----------|
| `--color-stroke-grid` | `hsl(0 0% 12%)` | `rgba(255,255,255,0.06)` grid lines |
| `--color-stroke-axis` | `hsl(0 0% 17%)` | `rgba(255,255,255,0.12)` baseline |
| `--color-stroke-secondary` | `hsl(0 0% 44%)` | `rgba(255,255,255,0.4)` |
| `--color-stroke-primary` | `hsl(0 0% 72%)` | `rgba(255,255,255,0.7)` |

### Kept as-is (opacity justified)

- `bg-black/60`, `bg-black/50` ‚Äî overlays on variable content
- `ring-white/50` ‚Äî focus ring accessibility
- `shadow-[..rgba(0,0,0,..)]` ‚Äî shadows need true opacity

## Implementation Sequence

### 1. Define tokens in `app/src/index.css`
Add all `--color-*` tokens to the `@theme` block. Remove unused `--color-accent`.

### 2. Migrate text colors (all components)
Replace `text-white/XX` ‚Üí `text-text-{semantic}` across all .tsx files. Also `fill-white/30` ‚Üí `fill-text-faint` in SVG text elements.

### 3. Migrate backgrounds (all components)
Replace `bg-white/[X.XX]` and `bg-white/XX` ‚Üí `bg-surface-{semantic}`. Special case: ControlBar backdrop uses `bg-surface-raised/85` (Tailwind 4 alpha modifier).

### 4. Migrate borders (all components)
Replace `border-white/[X.XX]` ‚Üí `border-border-{semantic}`.

### 5. Migrate SVG strokes (SaturationCurve, EffectiveSaturation, About)
Replace inline `stroke="rgba(...)"` ‚Üí `stroke="var(--color-stroke-{name})"`.

### 6. Migrate inline styles (SliderBase, SaturationSlider, ToggleSwitch)
Replace `rgba(255 255 255 / ...)` inline styles ‚Üí CSS variable references or Tailwind classes.

### Files to modify

- `app/src/index.css` ‚Äî token definitions
- `app/src/components/AppShell.tsx`
- `app/src/components/Header.tsx`
- `app/src/components/About.tsx`
- `app/src/components/controls/ControlBar.tsx`
- `app/src/components/controls/HueSlider.tsx`
- `app/src/components/controls/SaturationSlider.tsx`
- `app/src/components/controls/SliderBase.tsx`
- `app/src/components/controls/LightnessRange.tsx`
- `app/src/components/controls/AdjustmentSlider.tsx`

- `app/src/components/curves/EffectiveSaturation.tsx`

- `app/src/components/export/ExportDialog.tsx`
- `app/src/components/export/ExportPreview.tsx`
- `app/src/components/export/FormatSelector.tsx`

## Verification

1. `npm run build` ‚Äî must compile cleanly
2. `npm run lint` ‚Äî no errors
3. Visual check ‚Äî run dev server, compare all views (controls, grid, charts, export dialog, about dialog) against current state. Differences should be imperceptible.

Opacity-based text colors blend unpredictably with backgrounds and make the design system implicit rather than explicit. This pass defines a CSS custom property system with explicit HSL gray values, then migrates all components.

|-------|-------|----------|
| `--color-text-primary` | `hsl(0 0% 91%)` | `text-white/90`, `text-white/80` |
| `--color-text-secondary` | `hsl(0 0% 72%)` | `text-white/70` |
| `--color-text-tertiary` | `hsl(0 0% 60%)` | `text-white/60`, `text-white/55` |
| `--color-text-muted` | `hsl(0 0% 50%)` | `text-white/50`, `text-white/45` |

| Token | Value | Replaces |

| `--color-surface-active` | `hsl(0 0% 15%)` | `bg-white/10` |
| `--color-surface-active-hover` | `hsl(0 0% 20%)` | `bg-white/15`, `bg-white/20` |

### Borders (2)

| Token | Value | Replaces |
|-------|-------|----------|
| `--color-border-default` | `hsl(0 0% 12%)` | `border-white/[0.06]` |
| `--color-border-elevated` | `hsl(0 0% 15%)` | `border-white/[0.08]`, `border-white/10` |

### Strokes (4)

| Token | Value | Replaces |

| `--color-stroke-grid` | `hsl(0 0% 12%)` | `rgba(255,255,255,0.06)` grid lines |
| `--color-stroke-axis` | `hsl(0 0% 17%)` | `rgba(255,255,255,0.12)` baseline |
| `--color-stroke-secondary` | `hsl(0 0% 44%)` | `rgba(255,255,255,0.4)` |
| `--color-stroke-primary` | `hsl(0 0% 72%)` | `rgba(255,255,255,0.7)` |

### Kept as-is (opacity justified)

- `bg-black/60`, `bg-black/50` ‚Äî overlays on variable content
- `ring-white/50` ‚Äî focus ring accessibility
- `shadow-[..rgba(0,0,0,..)]` ‚Äî shadows need true opacity

## Implementation Sequence

### 1. Define tokens in `app/src/index.css`
Add all `--color-*` tokens to the `@theme` block. Remove unused `--color-accent`.

### 2. Migrate text colors (all components)
Replace `text-white/XX` ‚Üí `text-text-{semantic}` across all .tsx files. Also `fill-white/30` ‚Üí `fill-text-faint` in SVG text elements.

### 3. Migrate backgrounds (all components)
Replace `bg-white/[X.XX]` and `bg-white/XX` ‚Üí `bg-surface-{semantic}`. Special case: ControlBar backdrop uses `bg-surface-raised/85` (Tailwind 4 alpha modifier).

### 4. Migrate borders (all components)
Replace `border-white/[X.XX]` ‚Üí `border-border-{semantic}`.

### 5. Migrate SVG strokes (SaturationCurve, EffectiveSaturation, About)
Replace inline `stroke="rgba(...)"` ‚Üí `stroke="var(--color-stroke-{name})"`.

### 6. Migrate inline styles (SliderBase, SaturationSlider, ToggleSwitch)
Replace `rgba(255 255 255 / ...)` inline styles ‚Üí CSS variable references or Tailwind classes.

### Files to modify

- `app/src/index.css` ‚Äî token definitions
- `app/src/components/AppShell.tsx`
- `app/src/components/Header.tsx`
- `app/src/components/About.tsx`
- `app/src/components/controls/ControlBar.tsx`
- `app/src/components/controls/HueSlider.tsx`
- `app/src/components/controls/SaturationSlider.tsx`
- `app/src/components/controls/SliderBase.tsx`

- `app/src/components/palette/ColorSwatch.tsx`
- `app/src/components/palette/CopyFeedback.tsx`
- `app/src/components/palette/PaletteStrip.tsx`
- `app/src/components/export/ExportDialog.tsx`
- `app/src/components/export/ExportPreview.tsx`
- `app/src/components/export/FormatSelector.tsx`

## Verification

1. `npm run build` ‚Äî must compile cleanly
2. `npm run lint` ‚Äî no errors
3. Visual check ‚Äî run dev server, compare all views (controls, grid, charts, export dialog, about dialog) against current state. Differences should be imperceptible.

     1‚Üíimport { usePaletteStore } from "../../store/paletteStore";
     2‚Üíimport { SliderBase } from "./SliderBase";
     3‚Üí
     4‚Üí// Display: 0% = no curve (sMod=0), -1% to -100% = increasing curve strength.
     5‚Üí// Slider is linear in this percentage; we convert to/from sMod internally.
     6‚Üífunction pctToSMod(pct: number): number {
     7‚Üí  if (pct === 0) return 0;
     8‚Üí  return 2500 / -pct;
     9‚Üí}
    10‚Üí
    11‚Üífunction sModToPct(sMod: number): number {
    12‚Üí  if (sMod === 0) return 0;
    13‚Üí  return Math.round(-2500 / sMod);
    14‚Üí}
    15‚Üí
    16‚Üíexport function AdjustmentSlider() {
    17‚Üí  const sMod = usePaletteStore((s) => s.sMod);
    18‚Üí  const setSMod = usePaletteStore((s) => s.setSMod);
    19‚Üí
    20‚Üí  return (
    21‚Üí    <SliderBase
    22‚Üí      label="Adjustment"
    23‚Üí      value={sModToPct(sMod)}
    24‚Üí      min={-100}
    25‚Üí      max={0}
    26‚Üí      step={1}
    27‚Üí      onValueChange={(v) => setSMod(pctToSMod(v))}
    28‚Üí      formatValue={(v) => `${v}%`}
    29‚Üí    />
    30‚Üí  );
    31‚Üí}
    32‚Üí
```

     1‚Üíimport { useMemo, useState } from "react";
     2‚Üíimport { getSaturation } from "../../lib/palette";
     3‚Üíimport { usePaletteStore } from "../../store/paletteStore";
     4‚Üíimport { ToggleSwitch } from "../controls/ToggleSwitch";
     5‚Üíimport type { ColorEntry } from "../../types/palette";
     6‚Üíimport { W, H, PAD, PLOT_W, PLOT_H, toSvgX, toSvgY } from "./chartConstants";
     7‚Üí
     8‚Üíinterface EffectiveSaturationProps {
     9‚Üí  entries: ColorEntry[];
    10‚Üí}
    11‚Üí
    12‚Üíexport function EffectiveSaturation({ entries }: EffectiveSaturationProps) {
    13‚Üí  const [showDots, setShowDots] = useState(true);
    14‚Üí  const sMod = usePaletteStore((s) => s.sMod);
    15‚Üí  const saturation = usePaletteStore((s) => s.saturation);
    16‚Üí  const satMode = usePaletteStore((s) => s.satMode);
    17‚Üí
    18‚Üí  const { curvePath, baseLine, dots, gridLines } = useMemo(() => {
    19‚Üí    // Sample effective saturation at every integer lightness
    20‚Üí    const points: { x: number; y: number }[] = [];
    21‚Üí    for (let l = 0; l <= 100; l++) {
    22‚Üí      points.push({ x: l, y: saturation * getSaturation(l, sMod, satMode) });
    23‚Üí    }
    24‚Üí
    25‚Üí    const yMin = 0;
    26‚Üí    const yMax = 1; // fixed 0‚Äì100% range
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí    // Effective saturation curve path
    29‚Üí    const pathParts = points.map((p, i) => {
    30‚Üí      const cmd = i === 0 ? "M" : "L";
    31‚Üí      return `${cmd}${toSvgX(p.x).toFixed(2)},${toSvgY(p.y, yMin, yMax).toFixed(2)}`;
    32‚Üí    });
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí    // Flat baseline at base saturation
    35‚Üí    const baseY = toSvgY(saturation, yMin, yMax);
    36‚Üí    const baseLine = {
    37‚Üí      y: baseY,
    38‚Üí      label: `${Math.round(saturation * 100)}%`,
    39‚Üí    };
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí    // Dots at palette entry positions
    42‚Üí    const dots = entries.map((e) => {
    43‚Üí      const sK = getSaturation(e.lightness, sMod, satMode);
    44‚Üí      const effective = saturation * sK;
    45‚Üí      return {
    46‚Üí        cx: toSvgX(e.lightness),
    47‚Üí        cy: toSvgY(effective, yMin, yMax),
    48‚Üí        lightness: e.lightness,
    49‚Üí        hex: e.hex,
    50‚Üí      };
    51‚Üí    });
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí    // Horizontal grid lines at fixed 10% intervals
    54‚Üí    const gridLines = Array.from({ length: 11 }, (_, i) => {
    55‚Üí      const v = i * 0.1;
    56‚Üí      return { y: toSvgY(v, yMin, yMax), label: `${i * 10}%` };
    57‚Üí    });
    58‚Üí
    59‚Üí    return { curvePath: pathParts.join(""), baseLine, dots, gridLines };
    60‚Üí  }, [sMod, saturation, satMode, entries]);
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üí  const xGridValues = [0, 25, 50, 75, 100];
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üí  return (
    65‚Üí    <div className="mx-4 rounded-xl border border-white/[0.06] bg-white/[0.02] overflow-hidden">
    66‚Üí      <div className="px-4 py-3 border-b border-white/[0.06] flex items-center justify-between">
    67‚Üí        <h3 className="text-[13px] font-semibold text-white/70 tracking-wide">
    68‚Üí          Computed Saturation
    69‚Üí        </h3>
    70‚Üí        <ToggleSwitch label="steps" checked={showDots} onChange={setShowDots} />
    71‚Üí      </div>
    72‚Üí      <svg
    73‚Üí        viewBox={`0 0 ${W} ${H}`}
    74‚Üí        className="w-full h-auto"
    75‚Üí        role="img"
    76‚Üí        aria-labelledby="eff-sat-title"
    77‚Üí      >
    78‚Üí        <title id="eff-sat-title">Effective saturation chart</title>
    79‚Üí        {/* Grid lines - horizontal */}
    80‚Üí        {gridLines.map((g) => (
    81‚Üí          <g key={g.label}>
    82‚Üí            <line
    83‚Üí              x1={PAD.left}
    84‚Üí              y1={g.y}
    85‚Üí              x2={W - PAD.right}
    86‚Üí              y2={g.y}
    87‚Üí              stroke="rgba(255,255,255,0.06)"
    88‚Üí              strokeWidth={1}
    89‚Üí            />
    90‚Üí            <text
    91‚Üí              x={PAD.left - 8}
    92‚Üí              y={g.y + 3}
    93‚Üí              textAnchor="end"
    94‚Üí              className="text-[13px] fill-white/30 font-mono"
    95‚Üí            >
    96‚Üí              {g.label}
    97‚Üí            </text>
    98‚Üí          </g>
    99‚Üí        ))}
   100‚Üí
   101‚Üí        {/* Grid lines - vertical */}
   102‚Üí        {xGridValues.map((l) => (
   103‚Üí          <g key={l}>
   104‚Üí            <line
   105‚Üí              x1={toSvgX(l)}
   106‚Üí              y1={PAD.top}

   122‚Üí
   123‚Üí        {/* Axis labels */}
   124‚Üí        <text

   137‚Üí          transform={`rotate(-90, 12, ${PAD.top + PLOT_H / 2})`}
   138‚Üí        >
   139‚Üí          Saturation
   140‚Üí        </text>
   141‚Üí
   142‚Üí        {/* Base saturation line (flat / unadjusted) */}
   143‚Üí        <line

   152‚Üí

     1‚Üíimport { useCallback, useMemo, useRef, useState } from "react";
     2‚Üíimport * as Dialog from "@radix-ui/react-dialog";
     3‚Üíimport * as Popover from "@radix-ui/react-popover";
     4‚Üíimport { AnimatePresence, motion } from "framer-motion";
     5‚Üíimport chroma from "chroma-js";
     6‚Üíimport { Info } from "@phosphor-icons/react";
     7‚Üíimport { getSaturation } from "../lib/palette";
     8‚Üí
     9‚Üíconst DEFAULT_HUE = 220;
    10‚Üíconst REF_SMOD = 40;
    11‚Üíconst STRIP_STEPS = 9;
    12‚Üí
    13‚Üí// Saturation tuned for the midrange ‚Äî extremes lose chroma
    14‚Üíconst SAT_FOR_MID = 0.20;
    15‚Üí// Saturation tuned for the extremes ‚Äî midtones are over-saturated
    16‚Üíconst SAT_FOR_EXTREMES = 0.40;
    17‚Üí// Curve-modulated: base sat that produces balanced results
    18‚Üíconst SAT_CURVED = 0.35;
    19‚Üí
    20‚Üí// Swatch classification for 9 steps (indices 0-8)
    21‚Üí// "low" = not intense enough, "ok" = desired, "high" = too intense
    22‚Üítype SwatchMark = "low" | "ok" | "high" | null;
    23‚Üí
    24‚Üí// 20% strip: extremes not intense, center too intense, transition zones correct
    25‚Üíconst markForMid = (i: number): SwatchMark => {
    26‚Üí  if (i <= 1 || i >= 7) return "low";
    27‚Üí  if (i === 4) return "high";
    28‚Üí  return "ok";
    29‚Üí};
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí// 40% strip: extremes correct, midtones too intense
    32‚Üíconst markForExtremes = (i: number): SwatchMark => {
    33‚Üí  if (i <= 1 || i >= 7) return "ok";
    34‚Üí  return "high";
    35‚Üí};
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí// Curved: all correct
    38‚Üíconst markCurved = (): SwatchMark => "ok";
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí// Lightness step labels
    41‚Üíconst LIGHTNESS_LABELS = Array.from({ length: STRIP_STEPS }, (_, i) =>
    42‚Üí  Math.round(5 + i * (90 / (STRIP_STEPS - 1)))
    43‚Üí);
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí// Scale factor so parabola edges reach 50% sat (perceptual boundary)
    46‚Üíconst BOUNDARY_SCALE = 0.5 / getSaturation(LIGHTNESS_LABELS[0], REF_SMOD, "endpoint");
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üífunction buildStrips(hue: number) {
    49‚Üí  const zero: string[] = [];
    50‚Üí  const forMid: string[] = [];
    51‚Üí  const forExtremes: string[] = [];
    52‚Üí  const boundary: string[] = [];
    53‚Üí  const curved: string[] = [];
    54‚Üí  for (let i = 0; i < STRIP_STEPS; i++) {
    55‚Üí    const lInt = 5 + i * (90 / (STRIP_STEPS - 1)); // L5 through L95
    56‚Üí    const l = lInt / 100;
    57‚Üí    zero.push(chroma.hsl(hue, 0, l).hex());
    58‚Üí    forMid.push(chroma.hsl(hue, SAT_FOR_MID, l).hex());
    59‚Üí    forExtremes.push(chroma.hsl(hue, SAT_FOR_EXTREMES, l).hex());
    60‚Üí    boundary.push(
    61‚Üí      chroma

    97‚Üí            return (
    98‚Üí              <div key={`mark-${i}`} className="text-center leading-1.5">
    99‚Üí                {text && (
   100‚Üí                  <span className={`text-[8px] font-mono ${m === "ok" ? "text-[hsl(0_0%_65%)]" : "text-[hsl(0_0%_40%)]"}`}>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-26 22:57:07Z)**_

The Range element covers the hue gradient. When there's a custom `trackStyle` (like the hue gradient), the Range should be transparent so it doesn't obscure the track.

Before creating, check if a similar item already exists. If a close match is found, ask the user if they still want to create a new one.

## Scope Assessment

When the user requests a **TASK**, evaluate whether it is actually EPIC-sized before creating it. Look for these signals:

- Multiple distinct steps or deliverables in the description
- Multiple action verbs joined by "and" (e.g., "add auth and set up roles and integrate SSO")
- Work that spans different code areas or concerns
- Sub-steps that could be completed and shipped independently

**Quick test:** "Could someone meaningfully complete and ship part of this while the rest remains undone?" If yes ‚Üí likely an EPIC.

If EPIC signals are present, suggest an EPIC + sub-TASKs with a concrete breakdown. For example:

> "This looks like it has multiple independent deliverables. Want me to create an EPIC with separate TASKs for each piece instead?"

**This is a soft suggestion only.** If the user says "just make it a task," comply without pushback.

This assessment only applies to TASK creation ‚Äî do not second-guess EPICs, NOTEs, or other types.

## Arguments

The user provides the type and title: `/board:board-add task <title>`, `/board:board-add epic <title>`, etc.

If no type is given, ask the user what type of item to create.

## Determine Next ID

Run the ID assignment script:

```bash
bash /Users/grant/Documents/Projects/BoardPM/plugin/scripts/next-id.sh <TYPE> ./board
```

This scans all directories (including `board/archive/` and `.doc-ids`) and returns the next available 4-digit zero-padded ID.

## Create the File

Use the correct directory, frontmatter, and naming convention:

| Type | Directory | Filename Example |
|------|-----------|-----------------|
| task | `board/tasks/` | `TASK-0014_add-batch-processing.md` |
| epic | `board/planning/` | `EPIC-0009_batch-mode.md` |
| adr | `board/planning/` | `ADR-0002_chose-webgpu-over-wasm.md` |
| note | `board/planning/` | `NOTE-0001_explore-wasm-option.md` |
| reference | `board/reference/` | `REFE-0007_webgpu-overview.md` |
| resource | `board/resources/` | `RESO-0001_webgpu-article.md` |

## Frontmatter Templates

Task:
```yaml
---
type: task
status: backlog
priority: 2
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

Epic:
```yaml
---
type: epic
status: planning
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

ADR:
```yaml
---
type: adr
status: active
context_date: YYYY-MM-DD
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

Note:
```yaml
---
type: note
status: inbox
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

Reference:
```yaml
---
type: reference
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

Resource (ask user for URL):
```yaml
---
type: resource
status: inbox
url: <url>
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

## Naming Rules

- Convert title to kebab-case for filename
- Task titles MUST be imperative mood (start with a verb). If the user gives a noun phrase, rewrite it (e.g., "batch processing" -> "Add batch processing support")
- Use today's date for `created`

## After Creation

Show the file path and ask if the user wants to add more detail to the body.


ARGUMENTS: NOTE with these items

<command-message>impeccable:critique</command-message>
<command-name>/impeccable:critique</command-name>

_**User (2026-03-27 00:15:58Z)**_

Base directory for this skill: /Users/grant/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/impeccable/.claude/skills/critique

Conduct a holistic design critique, evaluating whether the interface actually works‚Äînot just technically, but as a designed experience. Think like a design director giving feedback.

**First**: Use the frontend-design skill for design principles and anti-patterns.

## Design Critique

Evaluate the interface across these dimensions:

### 1. AI Slop Detection (CRITICAL)

**This is the most important check.** Does this look like every other AI-generated interface from 2024-2025?

Review the design against ALL the **DON'T** guidelines in the frontend-design skill‚Äîthey are the fingerprints of AI-generated work. Check for the AI color palette, gradient text, dark mode with glowing accents, glassmorphism, hero metric layouts, identical card grids, generic fonts, and all other tells.

**The test**: If you showed this to someone and said "AI made this," would they believe you immediately? If yes, that's the problem.

### 2. Visual Hierarchy
- Does the eye flow to the most important element first?
- Is there a clear primary action? Can you spot it in 2 seconds?
- Do size, color, and position communicate importance correctly?
- Is there visual competition between elements that should have different weights?

### 3. Information Architecture
- Is the structure intuitive? Would a new user understand the organization?
- Is related content grouped logically?
- Are there too many choices at once? (cognitive overload)
- Is the navigation clear and predictable?

### 4. Emotional Resonance
- What emotion does this interface evoke? Is that intentional?
- Does it match the brand personality?
- Does it feel trustworthy, approachable, premium, playful‚Äîwhatever it should feel?
- Would the target user feel "this is for me"?

### 5. Discoverability & Affordance
- Are interactive elements obviously interactive?
- Would a user know what to do without instructions?
- Are hover/focus states providing useful feedback?
- Are there hidden features that should be more visible?

### 6. Composition & Balance
- Does the layout feel balanced or uncomfortably weighted?
- Is whitespace used intentionally or just leftover?
- Is there visual rhythm in spacing and repetition?
- Does asymmetry feel designed or accidental?

### 7. Typography as Communication
- Does the type hierarchy clearly signal what to read first, second, third?
- Is body text comfortable to read? (line length, spacing, size)
- Do font choices reinforce the brand/tone?
- Is there enough contrast between heading levels?

### 8. Color with Purpose
- Is color used to communicate, not just decorate?
- Does the palette feel cohesive?
- Are accent colors drawing attention to the right things?
- Does it work for colorblind users? (not just technically‚Äîdoes meaning still come through?)

### 9. States & Edge Cases
- Empty states: Do they guide users toward action, or just say "nothing here"?
- Loading states: Do they reduce perceived wait time?
- Error states: Are they helpful and non-blaming?
- Success states: Do they confirm and guide next steps?

### 10. Microcopy & Voice
- Is the writing clear and concise?
- Does it sound like a human (the right human for this brand)?
- Are labels and buttons unambiguous?
- Does error copy help users fix the problem?

## Generate Critique Report

Structure your feedback as a design director would:

### Anti-Patterns Verdict
**Start here.** Pass/fail: Does this look AI-generated? List specific tells from the skill's Anti-Patterns section. Be brutally honest.

### Overall Impression
A brief gut reaction‚Äîwhat works, what doesn't, and the single biggest opportunity.

### What's Working
Highlight 2-3 things done well. Be specific about why they work.

### Priority Issues
The 3-5 most impactful design problems, ordered by importance:

For each issue:
- **What**: Name the problem clearly
- **Why it matters**: How this hurts users or undermines goals
- **Fix**: What to do about it (be concrete)
- **Command**: Which command to use (prefer: /animate, /quieter, /optimize, /adapt, /clarify, /distill, /delight, /onboard, /normalize, /audit, /harden, /polish, /extract, /bolder, /critique, /colorize ‚Äî or other installed skills you're sure exist)

### Minor Observations
Quick notes on smaller issues worth addressing.

### Questions to Consider
Provocative questions that might unlock better solutions:
- "What if the primary action were more prominent?"
- "Does this need to feel this complex?"
- "What would a confident version of this look like?"

**Remember**:
- Be direct‚Äîvague feedback wastes everyone's time
- Be specific‚Äî"the submit button" not "some elements"
- Say what's wrong AND why it matters to users
- Give concrete suggestions, not just "consider exploring..."
- Prioritize ruthlessly‚Äîif everything is important, nothing is
- Don't soften criticism‚Äîdevelopers need honest feedback to ship great design

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-27 00:18:01Z)**_

---

## Design Critique: Chroma Curve

### Anti-Patterns Verdict: PASS

This does not read as AI-generated. No purple gradients, no glassmorphism, no hero metric cards, no identical card grids, no gradient text, no bounce animations. The dark theme is justified (color tool ‚Äî dark background lets generated colors speak). Geist is a strong font choice that avoids the Inter/Roboto trap. The restraint is genuine, not lazy ‚Äî there's a clear point of view here.

### Overall Impression

The tool feels **competent and honest** ‚Äî like something built by a person who actually uses color tools. The controls are dense but legible, the charts are genuinely useful (not decorative), and the generated palette is unmistakably the hero.

**Single biggest opportunity**: The page has a **vertical imbalance problem**. The left column (palette grid) ends at ~500px while the right column (charts) extends to ~950px, leaving a massive void. This dead space makes the layout feel unfinished rather than intentional.

### What's Working

1. **The palette is the star.** The near-black background, achromatic UI, and full-bleed palette strip all correctly defer to the generated colors. Nothing competes. This is the #1 design principle executed well.

2. **Control bar density.** Four sliders with mode toggles, value readouts, and a lock mechanism ‚Äî packed into one sticky row without feeling cramped. The new vertical divider between color/curve groups is the right call. The uppercase labels + mono values read cleanly.

3. **"What is this?" placement.** The info button sitting right after "Parabolic saturation correction" is excellent UX writing ‚Äî it answers the question the subtitle provokes, exactly where the eye lands. Good user-intent-driven flow.

### Priority Issues

**1. Vertical dead space in default layout**
- **What**: In row layout (the default), the swatch grid is ~220px tall while the charts column is ~700px. Below the grid is nothing.
- **Why it matters**: It looks like something broke or hasn't loaded. The asymmetry feels accidental, not designed. A first-time visitor might think the page is incomplete.
- **Fix**: Either let the swatch grid grow taller in row mode (the swatches are already `min-h-[240px]` when `tall` is true ‚Äî this works), or stack the charts below the grid at this aspect ratio, or add content to the left column (the palette strip could move here, or palette metadata like "8 steps, hue 220¬∞, HSL").

**2. The palette strip is an orphan**
- **What**: The thin gradient bar between the controls and the grid toolbar floats without label, border, or context. It's unclear what it represents or why it's separate from the grid below.
- **Why it matters**: New users won't understand that it's a full-resolution preview of all 101 lightness steps. It reads as a decorative element or rendering artifact.
- **Fix**: Either label it (a subtle "FULL SCALE" or step count), integrate it as the top edge of the grid section, or remove it if the grid already shows the palette sufficiently.

**3. "Compare" is invisible**
- **What**: The `‚ñ∏ Compare` disclosure in the Saturation Adjustment chart is `text-text-faint` (32% lightness) ‚Äî barely distinguishable from the background.
- **Why it matters**: This is a genuinely useful feature (unadjusted vs adjusted strips) that almost nobody will find. The comparison is one of the strongest selling points of the tool ‚Äî it proves the curve works.
- **Fix**: Bump to `text-text-subtle` at minimum, add a hover state, and consider using a Phosphor `CaretRight` icon. Could even default to open since the comparison strips are compact.

**4. Export button blends in**
- **What**: The Export button uses `bg-surface-active` ‚Äî the same visual weight as the format selector buttons inside the export dialog. It doesn't read as a primary action.
- **Why it matters**: Export is the terminal action of the entire tool ‚Äî the reason someone came here. It should be the most visually prominent interactive element after the palette itself.
- **Fix**: Give it a subtle border (`border-border-elevated`) or slightly brighter background. Don't go full CTA ‚Äî that would violate the "instrument, not toy" principle ‚Äî but it needs one notch more prominence.

**5. Swatch labels feel fragile**
- **What**: `grey-43` at `text-[11px] font-semibold` and `hsl(220deg 32.49% 43%)` at `text-[8px] font-mono` are hard to read on mid-tone swatches, especially where the contrast-detection auto-switches between light and dark text.
- **Why it matters**: The labels are the primary output a user copies. If they can't read them, they have to hover for the title tooltip or click to copy blind.
- **Fix**: Add a very subtle text shadow or background pill behind labels on mid-tone swatches (L30-70 range) where contrast is lowest. Or increase the minimum font size to 9px for the HSL value.

### Minor Observations

- **Hue preview dot** next to the hue slider has `border-border-elevated` which makes it look slightly disconnected from the track. It could share the slider track's visual language more closely.
- **Toggle switch "on" state** is hard to distinguish from "off" ‚Äî both are low-contrast grays. The thumb position communicates state, but the track color difference is subtle.
- **No favicon** ‚Äî 404 on every load. Small but professional polish.
- **The `max-w-5xl mx-auto` on the control bar** constrains it while the rest of the page is full-bleed. At very wide viewports the controls float in the center while the palette extends edge-to-edge. This is fine but could feel odd on ultrawide monitors.

### Questions to Consider

- **What if the Compare strips were always visible?** They're compact (two 24px bars) and they're the strongest visual argument for why the curve matters. Hiding them behind a disclosure buries the lede.
- **Does the palette strip add enough value to justify its screen space?** If users always work with the grid below, the strip is redundant. If it stays, it needs to earn its position with context.
- **What would "confident" look like for this tool?** Right now it's self-effacing almost to a fault ‚Äî everything is subdued. The palette output deserves more ceremony. What if the grid had slightly more visual weight, a subtle outer glow or shadow to frame it against the background?
- **Should the default swatch count be higher?** 8 swatches in row layout creates a lot of dead space. 12 or 16 would better fill the grid area and give a more impressive first impression.

# Impeccable /critique Review

Generated via `impeccable:critique` plugin ‚Äî 2026-03-26T23:15Z

## Anti-Patterns Verdict: PASS

Does not read as AI-generated. No purple gradients, glassmorphism, hero metric cards, identical card grids, gradient text, or bounce animations. Dark theme is justified (color tool ‚Äî dark background lets generated colors speak). Geist is a strong font choice. The restraint is genuine, not lazy ‚Äî clear point of view.

## Overall Impression

The tool feels **competent and honest** ‚Äî like something built by a person who actually uses color tools. Controls are dense but legible, charts are genuinely useful (not decorative), and the generated palette is unmistakably the hero.

**Single biggest opportunity**: Vertical imbalance ‚Äî left column (palette grid) ends at ~500px while right column (charts) extends to ~950px, leaving a massive void that makes the layout feel unfinished.

## What's Working

1. **The palette is the star.** Near-black background, achromatic UI, full-bleed palette strip all correctly defer to the generated colors. Nothing competes.
2. **Control bar density.** Four sliders with mode toggles, value readouts, and lock mechanism packed into one sticky row without feeling cramped. Vertical divider between color/curve groups is the right call.
3. **"What is this?" placement.** Info button sitting right after "Parabolic saturation correction" answers the question the subtitle provokes, exactly where the eye lands.

## Priority Issues

### 1. Vertical dead space in default layout
- **What**: In row layout (default), swatch grid is ~220px tall while charts column is ~700px. Below the grid is nothing.
- **Why it matters**: Looks like something broke or hasn't loaded. Asymmetry feels accidental, not designed.
- **Fix**: Let swatch grid grow taller in row mode, stack charts below the grid, or add content to left column (palette strip could move here, or palette metadata like "8 steps, hue 220, HSL").

### 2. Palette strip is an orphan
- **What**: Thin gradient bar between controls and grid toolbar floats without label, border, or context. Unclear what it represents or why it's separate from the grid below.
- **Why it matters**: New users won't understand it's a full-resolution preview of all 101 lightness steps. Reads as a decorative element or rendering artifact.
- **Fix**: Label it (subtle "FULL SCALE" or step count), integrate it as the top edge of the grid section, or remove if redundant.

### 3. "Compare" is invisible
- **What**: `Compare` disclosure in Saturation Adjustment chart uses `text-text-faint` (32% lightness) ‚Äî barely distinguishable from background.
- **Why it matters**: Genuinely useful feature (unadjusted vs adjusted strips) that almost nobody will find. The comparison is one of the strongest selling points ‚Äî it proves the curve works.
- **Fix**: Bump to `text-text-subtle` at minimum, add hover state, consider Phosphor `CaretRight` icon. Could default to open since strips are compact.

### 4. Export button blends in
- **What**: Export button uses `bg-surface-active` ‚Äî same visual weight as format selector buttons inside the export dialog. Doesn't read as a primary action.
- **Why it matters**: Export is the terminal action of the entire tool ‚Äî the reason someone came here.
- **Fix**: Subtle border (`border-border-elevated`) or slightly brighter background. Don't go full CTA (violates "instrument, not toy") but needs one notch more prominence.

### 5. Swatch labels feel fragile
- **What**: `grey-43` at `text-[11px]` and HSL value at `text-[8px]` are hard to read on mid-tone swatches, especially where contrast auto-switches between light and dark text.
- **Why it matters**: Labels are the primary output a user copies. If they can't read them, they have to hover for tooltip or click blind.
- **Fix**: Subtle text shadow or background pill behind labels on mid-tone swatches (L30-70 range). Or increase minimum font size to 9px for HSL value.

## Minor Observations

- **Hue preview dot** has `border-border-elevated` making it look slightly disconnected from the track.
- **Toggle switch on/off states** are hard to distinguish ‚Äî both low-contrast grays. Thumb position communicates state, but track color difference is subtle.
- **No favicon** ‚Äî 404 on every load.
- **`max-w-5xl mx-auto` on control bar** constrains it while rest of page is full-bleed. Could feel odd on ultrawide monitors.

## Questions to Consider

- **What if Compare strips were always visible?** They're compact (two 24px bars) and they're the strongest visual argument for why the curve matters. Hiding them behind a disclosure buries the lede.
- **Does the palette strip add enough value to justify its screen space?** If users always work with the grid below, the strip is redundant. If it stays, it needs to earn its position.
- **What would "confident" look like?** Right now it's self-effacing almost to a fault. The palette output deserves more ceremony ‚Äî subtle outer glow or shadow to frame the grid against the background?
- **Should the default swatch count be higher?** 8 swatches in row layout creates dead space. 12 or 16 would fill the grid better and give a more impressive first impression.

```
